notes: Game works well on Linux. But it doens't start at all on Proton, there is just the game.exe running with 100% of one CPU core and nothing happens then anymore.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of Mordor™

notes: Game works native on Linux, but not on Proton. There seems to be a bug in Wine https://appdb.winehq.org/objectManager.php?sClass=version&iId=31044#testdata.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of Mordor™

notes: Tried this game with new Proton Version 4.11-1 as the OpenGL native port runs really poorly for  me.  I wasn't expecting it to work as I have only read about it being borked under proton or  DXVK. I was surprised when the game  launched and ran ran for me, first time with zero tweaks. The graphics settings all defaulted to the  lowest settings and 540p, but I set 1920 x 1080 high in the menus and then ran the in-game benchmark.  The benchmark averaged 126 fps with a low of 29  fps and I did notice two stutters. This is still way better than I get with the native port.  I then played the game for 10 minutes and I haven't  noticed any issues or stuttering in normal gameplay yet.  I haven't yet played the game with proton long enough to rate it any higher than Gold.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of Mordor™

notes: Game works native on Linux (OpenGL). On Proton it works now since Proton 4.11 for the first time for me out of the box with DXVK (D3D11 to Vulkan translation). Tested with RADV and the new Mesa ACO compiler on Plasma Wayland.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of Mordor™

notes: Works out of the box. Performance is much better than the native OpenGL port. In the benchmark I get an average of 140 FPS on Ultra settings, however the game is capped at 100 FPS and never drops below.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of Mordor™

notes: Works perfectly out of the box and the performance is much better than the native version.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of Mordor™
